name: Vertumnus
description: Vertumnus, also known as Vortumnus, was a Roman god of seasons, change, plant growth, gardens, and fruit trees. He possessed the unique ability to change his form at will, appearing as a young soldier, a reaper, a fisherman, an old woman, or any other form he desired. This power allowed him to approach Pomona, the goddess of fruit trees and gardens, whom he courted relentlessly. He desired her hand in marriage, but she remained uninterested in her suitors. Disguised as an old woman, Vertumnus finally gained access to Pomona's orchard and, through flattery and a cautionary tale of a maiden who scorned love, convinced her to accept his proposal. He revealed his true form, and Pomona, impressed by his persistence and charm, agreed to marry him. Vertumnus was associated with the cycle of the seasons and the abundance of the earth, ensuring a bountiful harvest for the Roman people. He was particularly venerated by gardeners and fruit growers, who sought his blessing for their crops. His festival, the Vertumnalia, was celebrated on August 13th. His statue stood in the Vicus Tuscus, near the Roman Forum, a testament to his importance in Roman religious life. He represents the transformative power of nature and the rewards of perseverance.
subtitle: God of Seasons, Change, and Gardens

keyMyths.title: Vertumnus and Pomona
keyMyths.description: Vertumnus, disguised as an old woman, woos Pomona with flattery and a story about the dangers of rejecting love. He reveals his true form, and Pomona accepts his proposal.

keyMyths.title: The Transformations of Vertumnus
keyMyths.description: Vertumnus uses his ability to shapeshift to gain access to Pomona's orchard. He transforms into various figures, including a soldier, a reaper, and an old woman.

keyMyths.title: Vertumnalia Festival
keyMyths.description: The Vertumnalia was celebrated on August 13th to honor Vertumnus. It involved offerings and prayers for a successful harvest and the continuation of the seasons.

sources.author: Ovid
sources.description: A collection of mythological narratives, including the story of Vertumnus and Pomona.
sources.text: A collection of mythological narratives, including the story of Vertumnus and Pomona.

sources.author: Propertius
sources.description: A collection of poems that mention Vertumnus and his transformative abilities.
